subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear table of contents with numbered headings and logical subdivision of sections
Figures and tables placed appropriately after introduction in text
Recognisable investigative thread linking personal context, model derivation, data collection and conclusion
weaknesses: Page-long blocks of raw calculations and duplicate tables interrupt the flow
Tangential background material and repeated definitions reduce conciseness
Inconsistent reference formatting and minor navigability issues in the table of contents
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed, with a well-organised structure and clear subdivisions (band 3). However, excessive repetition, copy-paste artefacts, and irrelevant digressions prevent it from reaching the âexceptionalâ level. A score of 3 reflects strong organisation and logical flow but acknowledges the impact of recurring conciseness issues.

strengths: Consistent use of defined variables, correct application of calculus notation in differential equation derivation
Effective employment of multiple representations (equations, data tables, overlay graphs)
Logical progression of mathematical steps and clear linkage between model and data
weaknesses: Minor notation inconsistencies (misplaced subscripts, missing brackets)
Graphs lack error bars and fully labelled axes for precision
Occasional long calculation dumps without commentary dilute clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates good command of mathematical language and notation, uses different representations effectively, and presents a clear logical structure (band 3). However, the presence of minor inconsistencies, insufficient labelling, and absence of uncertainty depiction prevent an âexcellentâ rating. Hence a score of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Clear evidence of independent choice in adapting and applying Newtonâs cooling model
Personal narrative (family context, drinking-temperature threshold) motivating methodological decisions
Good creativity in linking physical theory to an everyday context and designing homemade experiments
weaknesses: Mathematical methods remain standard without significant extension beyond textbook techniques
Limited deeper personal reflection on challenges or insights during the investigation
No highly innovative or novel approach beyond adapting published methods
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant independent thinking, a clear personal approach, and good creativity (band 2). It falls short of the âoutstandingâ level, as the mathematics remains largely routine and personal reflections are not deeply developed. A score of 2 accurately reflects the studentâs level of engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on modelling assumptions, limitations, and comparison of experimental outcomes
Thoughtful discussion of results in terms of cooling-rate differences
Clear evaluation of implications and suggestions for future improvements
weaknesses: Lacks quantitative error analysis or uncertainty propagation to critically assess model fit
Reflection remains descriptive with limited probing of why assumptions fail
Minimal discussion of statistical validity or deeper critical insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present throughout and provides thoughtful analysis of results and clear evaluation of outcomes (band 2). However, the absence of quantitative uncertainty estimates and deeper critical examination prevents a higher rating. A score of 2 therefore best fits the observed quality of reflection.

strengths: Correct derivation and application of Newtonâs cooling differential equation to real data
Use of calculus and basic statistics is fully appropriate to SL level
Mathematics is used effectively to predict temperatures and compare cooling methods
weaknesses: Omission of absolute value notation in integration step
Inconsistent ambient temperature values and unit specification for the cooling constant k
No uncertainty propagation or more advanced regression analysis to strengthen results
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant and effectively applied throughout the investigation. Derivations and computations are at the expected SL level, with good understanding and only minor errors (e.g., notation, units). This justifies a band 4 score for Use of Mathematics, recognising effective and mostly accurate use of mathematics while noting room for minor improvements.

strandSelections.reasoning: Most steps in the differential-equation solution are explained and the reader can trace how data are split and averaged.  Nevertheless, long calculation dumps without commentary and occasional algebra slips reduce clarity.  The work therefore shows good rather than excellent logical structure and communication.

The derivation is essentially correct and the data satisfy the model reasonably.  Nevertheless, algebraic mishaps (missing brackets) and an absence of uncertainty calculations indicate understanding that is âgood with minor errorsâ rather than thorough and completely correct.  Band 3 is therefore selected.

The exploration employs equations, derivations, data tables and various line graphs to represent information.  Each representation supports the discussion (e.g. using the theoretical model curve against observed temperatures, overlaying metal-spoon vs ice-cube curves).  The selection is effective but not exceptionalâgraphs lack error bars and axes labelling is basicâso âeffective useâ (band 3) is appropriate.

The student follows a recognisable investigative thread: personal context â derivation of a cooling model â data collection â analysis â conclusion.  Although sections occasionally digress (long quotations, repeated definitions), the argument usually advances in a sensible order and references earlier work.  Transitional sentences link most subsections, so the reader can see why each mathematical step appears where it does.  The reasoning therefore meets âcoherent and logically developedâ rather than merely âsome coherence,â but falls short of âexcellentâ because irregular phrasing, tangential commentary and occasional loss of focus weaken the logical flow.

The investigation demonstrates creativity in linking thermodynamics, calculus and an everyday context, and in constructing homemade experiments.  Still, the analytical techniques (averages, basic exponential fit) are fairly routine, so creativity is good but not exceptional.

The student compares observed vs theoretical temperatures and quantifies cooling rates, which constitutes thoughtful analysis.  Nonetheless, statistical validity, error propagation and model fit metrics are absent, so the analysis is not âdeep and insightful.â  Band 2 is therefore appropriate.

Outcomes are evaluated (metal spoon judged superior; limitations and future work suggested).  The evaluation acknowledges uncertainties but does not quantify them or project specific improvements.  This aligns with âclear evaluation of outcomes and implicationsâ (band 2).

Reflection appears in âData Evaluation,â âConcluding Remarksâ and âLimitations.â  The student considers why the metal spoon might be preferable and notes modelling assumptions, showing meaningful reflection, yet the commentary remains descriptive and lacks probing questions or alternative interpretations.  Hence band 2 (âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ) fits best.

Variables are defined in tables, calculus symbols are employed correctly in the differential equation and solution, and units are usually attached.  Minor inconsistencies (e.g. misplaced subscripts, the expression âT0 â Ts eâ¦â with missing brackets) occur, preventing fully accurate usage, yet overall the mathematical language is appropriate and mostly consistent, justifying band 3.

A personal narrative from family experience motivates the task, and decisions such as the 60 Â°C drinking threshold and data-splitting approach reflect a personal way of tackling the problem.  These elements meet âclear evidence of personal approachâ but do not rise to âhighly personal and innovative.â

The IA runs to over 40 pages and repeats definitions, tables and full calculation lists that could have been sampled or summarised.  Several paragraphs stray into physics background that does not inform the later comparison.  Despite these excesses, most of the material is still related to the cooling problem, so the strand satisfies âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements,â matching band 2.

Deriving Newtonâs cooling formula, applying it to predict temperatures and computing average cooling rates are relevant and correctly linked to the research question.  The mathematics is suitable for SL but limited in breadth (no regression, differential-equation solving software, or uncertainty propagation).  The work thus satisfies ârelevant mathematics used appropriately,â which corresponds to band 3.

The student adapts a published Newton cooling model and decides to compare two folk techniques, showing noticeable independent choice and some manipulation (e.g., devising split-dataset rate analysis).  However, the mathematics itself remains standard and there is no significant extension beyond textbook material, so the evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstanding.â

A full table of contents, numbered headings and clear subdivision of theory / experiment / reflection indicate deliberate structure.  Figures and tables are placed after the text that introduces them, and the appendix is referenced.  However, page-long blocks of raw rate calculations split across pages, duplicate data tables and evident copy-paste artefacts interrupt the flow.  Consequently the organisation is âwell-organised with clear structureâ but not âexceptionally well organisedâ.

The solution of a first-order separable differential equation, use of exponentials and basic statistics are fully commensurate with SL expectations but not beyond.  Therefore the level is âcommensurate with course level,â earning band 3.

annotations.comment: Graphs lack error bars to indicate measurement uncertainty. Including error bars would strengthen the analysis by showing confidence in the fit.
Axes on all graphs should include clear labels and units (e.g. Â°C vs minutes). This improves interpretability and mathematical communication.
The table of contents contains duplicated entries and blank rows which disrupts navigability. Consolidate identical items and remove empty lines to enhance organizational clarity.
The introduction digresses into personal travel and air-conditioning context that is not directly linked to the cooling model. Focus on relevant background to maintain conciseness and relevance.
The hypothesis statement is lengthy and lacks mathematical clarity. Condense the wording and express key predictions succinctly using defined variables (e.g. cooling rate k) for precision.

In the equilibrium inequality \(\mathrm{Temp}_{tea} > \mathrm{Temp}_{equilibrium} > \mathrm{Temp}_{ice}\), the subscript spelling is inconsistent (âeaulbriumâ). Correct variable names and ensure brackets when needed.
Table 2 lists k with unit âconstant,â which is not a physical unit. Define k as dimensionless or specify its units (e.g. sâ»Â¹) to clarify its mathematical role.
The text alternates between ÎU and dE/dt for internal energy, causing confusion. Use a single consistent symbol throughout to represent the rate of change of energy.
The rearrangement from Q=ÎUâW to dE/dt=âQ skips clarifying sign conventions for heat flow and work. Explicitly state why W=0 and how Qâs sign is defined.
The separation of variables step could be clearer. Show explicitly dividing both sides by \(T - T_s\) to reach \(dT/(T - T_s) = -k dt\) instead of skipping intermediate algebra.
In the integration step you use \(\int1/(T - T_s)dT\) but omit absolute values. The correct antiderivative is \(\ln|T - T_s|\). Include |Â·| to ensure mathematical accuracy.
The exponentiation explanation references \(e^{i\theta}\) incorrectly. Use \(e^{\ln x}=x\) to justify removing the log for clarity.
The ambient temperature Ts is later recorded as 31Â°C but earlier graphs show 29Â°C. Reconcile these values consistently in your model and data.
The data table on page 13 shows only headers and no values. Include the observed and theoretical values in the table or refer clearly to the appendix.
No quantitative error analysis or uncertainty propagation is included. Incorporate uncertainty estimates to critically evaluate model fit and experimental precision.
Reflection sections describe limitations but lack deeper insight into why assumptions fail or how they could affect results. Probe consequences of each assumption.
References are formatted inconsistently (e.g., missing dates or italics). Adopt a single consistent style (APA or MLA) to strengthen presentation.
Include a brief personal reflection on what challenges or insights emerged during the investigation to demonstrate deeper personal engagement.
The mathematical concepts table is informative but overly verbose. Summarize each concept in one sentence to improve conciseness and readability.
The appendixâs raw data tables span many pages, overwhelming the narrative. Summarise key data in main text and relocate full tables to an online supplement.
When splitting data by drinking temperature threshold, highlight ranges numerically rather than by color to ensure clarity in monochrome printing.
The average rate uses n=52 intervals for 52 data points; correct denominator should be number of intervals (nâ1) to avoid bias in rate calculation.
The formula \(\overline A=\sum T_4/\sum T_4\) is incorrect; average should be \(\sum T_4/n\). Correct the expression for clarity.
The experimental photographs lack captions indicating date, time, and variable values. Add concise captions for each image to document data collection conditions.
